description: Goal: stop encoding publisher ownership and payload movement in the same (FactKey, bool) shape. A job owns a set of output facts for this run; payload/content movement is a separate change signal. Signal: facts_test and scheduler_test prove stable republication preserves revision, output retraction remains exact, and a fact can remain present while publisher contributions or readiness move independently. Strategy: replace JobEffects.outputs Vec<(FactKey,bool)> with explicit owned outputs plus changed content facts, update FactTable replacement around ownership first, and remove the old tuple shape from compiler2 code and docs.

comments.text: Split compiler2 fact publication into separate output ownership and content-change channels. Verified with cargo test --lib compiler2::facts_test:: -- --nocapture, cargo test --lib compiler2::scheduler_test:: -- --nocapture, cargo test --lib compiler2::source_publish_test:: -- --nocapture, cargo test --lib compiler2::drive_test:: -- --nocapture, and cargo test --lib compiler2:: -- --nocapture.

title: Compiler2 fact engine: split fact ownership from content movement
